description: Overall we are very pleased with the decision we made
author: Fred P.
pros: Multiple functionality is streamlines a day-to-day workflow and allows us to analyse what’s going on from so many angles
cons: Learning to use this software took far too long and was not well administered. People teaching uscdidn’t seem to have a grasp on our business. No this happened a year or two before Deltek took over, I was not administered by them.

description: It’s really useful and helps manage the day but a recommendation would be to keep functionality limited to critical for each team so that users don’t become overwhelmed
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Data management, time line and project set up is all useful and makes managing my day easier
cons: There is an overly large amount of functionality that can sometimes confuse users to what the product should be doing

description: Ultimately, the software was mostly used for tracking hours on tasks and assigning them to the right client. But it could get tedious depending on how many clients you have or if a client had subproject codes which necessitated using dozens of rows to track keep track of hours and billing. However, this would be more agency and client-specific issues. As a task and hour tracker, it gets the job done.
author: Hubert R.
pros: The program gives the ability to pin project codes for clients which means that if you have static clients you don't have to dig through codes week to week before beginning to insert hours. This removes a tedious part of hourly project tracking, unlike some other programs that require you to reinsert them week to week. The program also has a comment section that allows you to describe the tasks that were worked on, so it makes it easy to recall what exactly was being worked if you need to look back at your hours or track down projects.
cons: The navigability of the software can be confusing at times. Especially if you are mostly just using the program for work task tracking. Often, I would have messages that I wasn't sure how to get rid of or messages in the inbox making you think there was something urgent that needed to be taken care of but was really for just something from the previous week like clicking on a button to resubmit approvals or things of that nature.

author: martin C.
pros: excellent resource planning and project management, good summary information for PMs. easy to use. HTML interface is much better than Silverlight now.
cons: a lot of the reports are unclear, where figures are derived from. There are too many of them to be of real use. It would be great if reports were tailored to company requirements as part of set up. As an engineering firm and not an agency, we feel that sometimes our core requirements are disregarded, or overlooked.

author: Oliver E.
pros: This has a comprehensive list of features for project management.
cons: There is a lot to learn - onboarding can take a while.

review_title: The tool that best adapts to our needs, very difficult to implement
description: That the team is familiar with the infinity of options that it offers you is an impossible task, and although the consultants have been very friendly, we are not managing to integrate the tool into our daily life.
author: Diana J.
pros: I looked for an ERP that could adapt to the needs of a service agency, and among a benchmark of more than 15 options we opted for a workbook for its price and proposal.
cons: Its implementation has been very complicated, so much so that we are not yet at an optimal point in the process and not very satisfied with this.

review_title: Waiting for the promised UX / performance improvements
description: I still see the huge potential of this tool, but am not sure when in the future we will be able to realise the true potential
author: Anna B.
pros: It is one central place for all data relating to managing projects, and has some good views in the JobsList and Costs tracking. It is also great to be able to tailor your view (to a point)
cons: There are so many bugs / performance issues throughout the system, which means we don't use it to the full extent. I cannot wait for the promised UX improvements to come to reality, but we are still waiting. There is so much great data in the system, but it is very difficult to get customised exports in one report so current analysis is still extreme admin heavy, downloading and collating multiple exports which is far from ideal.

description: Easy overall visibility on the workload of a team. Easy modifications to the planning daily.
author: Vladimir V.
pros: Good visibility on the workload of the team and easy changes regarding assigned hours. Création of projects.
cons: Not easily adaptable to specific needs of a team, organisation. Problèm with our web server, Many delays during the day within the software. The interface could be improved for a better and more modern and fun user expérience.

review_title: Good but you need a lot of training
description: Overall once you get through the hiccups it's fine just takes a while.
author: Dan P.
pros: Workbook does it all, from expenses to time-off requests to time sheets to resourcing. It really does everything at once.
cons: It takes a long time to learn. We spent weeks training on it then weeks implementing it. Not the easiest to master, a lot of settings to toggle that make it not the most user friendly for a big office.

description: Time tracking at work
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I enjoy how responsive the Workbook team is to assisting in custom builds to accommodate certain business structures
cons: The software isn't as user friendly as I'd like, and the system can be fairly confusing (and not particularly intuitive) if you haven't received proper training.
author_industry: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Quick to learn and master for admins and users. Ideal for small and medium sized business.
description: Having used the software over an 7 year period I believe it to be very intuitive and ever changing. It is easy for new users to get acquainted with the interface and master the basics. Workbook fulfills all our basic need for an ERP system, and any other needs the customer support are usually able to accommodate in various custom-made reports. There might be a lack of business intelligence, but the vast and flexible data dumps easily make up for this.
author: John Ø.

review_title: Indispensable software to use for an effective working day in the consulting industry
description: For many years WorkBook has been Peoplegroup's anchor to structure our work, and it have contributed to a thorough and valuable input about our company's current status and expectations for the future. Specially the many types of profitability reports gives us an exact picture of all aspects of our business that allows us to keep our high standard.
author: Jens G.

review_title: Overall i believe Workbook has great potential, but we have not been able to harness it yet.
description: Workbook does a great job of collecting user data, but is weak on reporting. I feel there is a wealth of info, but unable to unlock it. We have struggled with the 'exceptions' - correcting user errors, subscriptions. Finance module is unusable in its current state.
author: Graham F.

review_title: Very good product! all in one place
description: After a long search in a sea of options we have found all we were looking for in one place. Super convenient and allows full visibility and traceability. Very good product, recommended!!!
author: Daniel G.

review_title: The best ERP and projekt tool ever
description: Mediegruppen has been working with similar systems for 18 years. Workbook is even better, and is a mayer part of our daily work, life and succes.
author: Jacob E.

description: But there is room to improve.
author: Anders H.

description: Pros: We have used 3 different PM-tools previously and about 20 trial versions of different PM-tools previously before Workbook. No other tool keeps their promise like WorkBook when it comes of being the full solution. Most other tools can't even do half of the features that WorkBook handles. They really have an extensive solution for all parts of our business; from CRM, to Cost calculations, Quotation, resource planning, time reporting, flex-hour handling to billing and accounting and analysis all in the same interface. The platform also handles global multi-regional clients and billing in a good way. A need for us since we are handling clients with multiple debtors scattered across different countries. I know no other platform which handles that without being a very expensive custom made platform. WorkBook easily handles different regions with individual currency, payment cycles and VAT rules, making a lot of the process automated. Just set up your client sub debtors and it's all there. Cons: WorkBook is very extensive, so I would not recommend this product to smaller companies. WorkBook suits companies where there's a resource available for handling Admin / Traffic of such a tool. So if you don't have that setup, then it is not a tool for you. In other words, probably a solution for minimum 10+ employee companies. Summary: WorkBook is the only one out there we found that handles pretty much everything you need for Admin of an enterprise and at an attractive price. But it is not for micro-companies with 3-10 employees.
author: Erik T.

description: We were looking for an intergrated CRM / Pipeline / Job Costing and finance system. We had conducted intensive research on the options available on the market. Our challenge was to have realtime information that allowed us to react swiftly and in an accurately informed way to work in process, not only on the external spend but the recording of time and the financial impact it was having on the project and therefore the company. Workbook was a last minute option and we decided to have a demo in order to confirm our original choice but that demo changed our opinion and workbook proved it had arrived at a place the other systems were looking to achieve. It is intuitive and easy to use that the key component areas of timesheets and budgets that the majority of staff use have been adopted enthusiastically. The move from our old systems hasn;t been without teething problems but customer service has been great and they have responded to all the requests and challenges we have thrown at them. There are still some challenges around certain elements in the finance section, the replacement of Sage 100 in our case, but these are now under development as thier model is to constantly seek to improve the product. Importantly we now have the data from projects at our fingertips and this has proved invaluable and will in the long term it will have a positive financial effect on our business. I would recommend this to any business that is fast moving, needs to record time accurately and respond to clients changes and needs quickly.
author: Dolan H.

description: We've been running our consulting and accounting business for over 20 years. Most of the time we used various project management, time tracking and billing systems. Last year we decided to introduce WorkBook and replace the previous system we used. As we knew what we needed, the implementation was fast (approx. 5 working days). The new solution was easily accepted by all people in our organization and we didn't experience any challenges in this area, opposite to previous systems. Some features that were important for us: - multi-language interface, - invoicing in many currencies and languages, - direct access to the database allowing easy creation of own reports and integration with other systems we use, - easy administration, - ticket system for quality management system, - conversations that are very useful not only for communication, but also making reports of the minutes and tracking various project activities. When we started using the Polish interface we found many translations inaccurate or even funny. We were not very happy about it, however the translation tool allows us not only to adjust the translations to the Polish language, whenever we find a mistake, but also to the nomenclature we use internally. The only thing we miss is a wiki module that would allow to describe the internal processes and tracking their changes. The customer service answers very fast, what is not to overestimate these days - 5 stars in each category.
author: Tomasz W.

review_title: not userfriendly, extremely heavy to use, confusing trainslation, confusing set-up
description: The pros for the software is that is includes both CRM, Pipeline, Finance, RM and PM modules. The contras are unfortunately many. For everyday use and as working Tool, it is absolutely not user-friendly. Finance people, the way they think and Work, might be useful. However, sales and project managers think and Work completely different. Therefore the current set-up of workbook is far from being the best available software on the market. The customer service is not wokring smoothly, ( a kind of stiff - compeared to another customer service experines in business life) the ticket system is lacking the possibility of seeing WHO is included in the ticket correspondance, and also the possibility of adding/deleting participants from the list . Because of the complexity of the software, customer customized solutions are unfortunately very limited.
author: Erika Katalin M.

description: We have created more efficient business processes, reduced our dependence on emails and paper, and are now able to capture time entries for everyone in our company.
author: Jeff B.
pros: WorkBook is extremely easy to use, has all of the features we need to creative project management, and they provide us with amazing customer service!
cons: That I didn't know about it 10 years ago.

author: Vladimir V.
pros: Easy to schedule and manage time. Easy to create projects and tasks.
cons: The software is not flexible in term of features. Hard to personnalize for specific needs.

description: We are a full service marketing agency who started using WorkBook in the last year. The software has been great at addressing a lot of our project management needs such as resource scheduling and project timelines and management. We started using the software in the midst of their transition from V8 to V9 which made implementation a little difficult, as we had to switch between two versions and ran into product bugs. However, since the release of V9 a lot of those initial issues have been addressed and we run into much fewer issues now. The software is very robust so implementation can be daunting, but our implementation specialist and the support team have been great throughout the whole process. We've noticed a big difference in a lot of our internal processes since switching to WorkBook and I would recommend the product to other agencies and companies that are looking for a project management solution. The software has a lot of other capabilities beyond project management including a CRM, which is one of the key reasons we chose WorkBook over other project management platforms.
author: Micaiah N.

review_title: Top notch features. Perfect project management tool for agencies.
description: We have been using Workbook for years. Heavily used others in the past. Nothing like it. When we chose them, I looked at their client list and was impressed.. big agency names and there is a reason for it. Researched on the other side of the pond to find our tool. Investigated small and large agencies and the response was pretty much unanimous: Workbook. Pros: Clean nice looking and elegant. Offer modular pricing and easy to adopt solution. VERY easy to implement as their team will actually spend time with you and do it for/with you to ensure that you are set from the get go. Really works from day one. Great Todo all customizable and easy project management tools for the agency folks. Simple interface. Mobile apps. HTML5 looks great with kanban task view an mobile access. Simplified CRM (we got rid of SF), project management & accounting. A few weeks realistic to implement. Awesome team too and they developed custom features for us. Cons: Occasional Danish word in the interface. Limited custom reporting. Dashboards and expense app not ready yet. No custom widgets.
author: Jesse A. P.

review_title: Excellent PM tool with all things integrated
description: WorkBook is a great all-in-one solution and there is nothing like it on the current market. We used to have multiple systems for different departments, but WorkBook has everything integrated. We now have time tracking, project management, resource management, budgeting, forecasting (incl. pipelines), CRM and all finances in WorkBook. We are in the process of setting up the also-integrated support system. The WorkBook implementation team helped us get started and customized everything for us. We were up and running in three weeks! They were also good to implement features that we need for our projects. Cons are the limited reporting in finance, but you can export everything to Excel, so we just manage the data ourselves. It's also a bit hard to get started and get to know the system (even though training is part of implementation), but once you get the hang of it, it's all pretty straight-forward. Tons of features - I would definitely recommend WorkBook to everybody! But the best part is that you only pay for what you need!
author: Stephen W.
